## Data Analysis

In order to test the validity of the suggestion to include pre-lecture videos in course content. I performed a series of analysis utilizing the survey data collected from the students in COMP110. Specficially, I analyzed two main questions. First, whether students believe that optional pre-lecture videos prepare students for the content of each lecture. Next, the students assesment of their current understanding of course content. Utilizing a scatterplot, boxplot, and bar graph the data was analyzed for trends in the relationship between pre-lecture videos and understanding. The scatterplot and boxplot yeilded inconclusive results due to the lack of observable trend between the variables. However, the bar graph depicted a trend of increasing interest in having access to pre-lecture videos. Overall, these results remain inconclusive. While in the bar plot we see an observable trend of increased interest in having pre-lecture videos offered the scatter plot and box plot yeild inconclusive results. Therefore this data does not confirm or deny the impact of pre lecture videos on student understanding. More specific questions are necessary to collect more robust data on this topic. I would suggest adding follow up questions regarding the availability of pre-lecture videos and the direct impact on student learning.
